titleStmt.title: SII 30.54: original edition by N. Marxia Gandhi, A. Padmavathy and S. Swaminathan
No. 54. (A.R. No. 131 of 1915.) Tirumurugaṉpūṇḍi, Avinashi Taluk, Coimbatore District Muruganātha Temple-on the south and east walls of the Goddess shrine in the same temple. 14th c. A.D.

summary.p: Damaged. This inscription praises the act of making and protecting the gifts. It records the donation of money to the temple by a dancing girl namely Nāṟpatteṇṇāyira Naṅgai, which was entrusted with the temple trustees and mendicants (kōvaṇavar, tāṉattār and virabhatṟas) of the temple. It also records some privileges given to her while worshipping the deity, such as showing flywhisk, mirror and singing songs with gesture etc.,

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
taṇṭiśvaraṉ Ōlai cākarañcūḻ vaiyyakattut taṇṭiyavaran karumamārāykai paṇṭeyaṟañ ceytāṉ (ceytāṉ) Aṟaṅkāttāṉ pātam tappāmal ceṉṉimel vaittu Aruḷāl Āti taṇṭeśvaraṉ Ātecam nam tirumaṭaiviḷākattu tevaraṭiyār
rukaṉpūṇṭi Āḷuṭaiyaṉāyaṉār ci paṇṭārattu Aṭi Uṟaiyār kovaṇavarkkum tāṉattārkkum virapattirarkkum kuṭutta yumRead- paṇam 3 10 Āka yum 8 10 Ippaṇam Eṇpatum Oṭukkukaiyil nām Ivarkaḷukku varicai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu nāṟpatteṇṇāyira naṅkai
mum tiruveṇcāmaraiyum tirukaṇṇāṭiyuṅ kāḷāñcamum meṟi cevikka kaṭavarākavum cevikkumiṭattu AiñcāmaṟaiyumRead- veñcāmaraiyumkaāṭṭi tiruppāṭṭukkum kaikāṭṭa kaṭavāḷākavum maṟṟum Eppeṟpaṭṭatuvum Urimai kuṭitaṉap peṟum
Digital edition of SII 30.54 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
